completed: Fetched origin/main and verified clean local main matches the remote baseline
Selected R90-14 as the highest-priority dependency-ready increment
Confirmed the initial C connection sends hello before packet and heartbeat traffic
Confirmed the UDS_ERR_PIPE reconnect path opens a replacement connection without resending hello
Recorded the compatibility impact and explicit unblock condition in the rolling roadmap
Received explicit authority to make every C replacement connection send hello before packet or heartbeat traffic
Added reconnect_with_hello and routed packet- and heartbeat-detected disconnects through it
Enforced exactly one connection-local hello before packet or heartbeat frames and matched heartbeat session IDs
Closed only state-violating connections and counted every violation exactly once
Added direct packet-before-hello, heartbeat-before-hello, duplicate-hello, mismatched-session, isolation, capacity-reuse, concurrent-session, reconnect, and cancellation coverage
Passed 20 focused C socket and receiver race runs, the full native race and ASan suites, E2E smoke, documentation, knowledge, JSON, and diff checks
Passed post-fetch knowledge validation and verified the exact Vault note, full index, and MOC

resolved_blocker.summary: The checked-in C reconnect path does not satisfy hello-first ordering on replacement connections
evidence: capture/src/main.c packet_handler calls uds_reconnect after UDS_ERR_PIPE without calling uds_send_hello
capture/src/uds_sender.c uds_reconnect only opens the replacement socket
resolved_blocker.impact: Receiver-only enforcement would close a valid checked-in capture reconnect on its next packet or heartbeat
resolved_blocker.resolution: The user explicitly authorized the C reconnect-handshake change on 2026-07-20

remaining_risks: Connection session state must remain local across concurrent clients
Every state violation must close only its connection and increment decode errors once
resume_instructions: R90-14 is complete at 3f999e497393e79d5629653055d60514791c2281. Do not repeat its implementation, validation, push, or Vault synchronization. R90-15 is the next ready increment: reject incompatible existing SQLite schemas before writable initialization while preserving compatible, empty, and missing database behavior. Do not create v0.1.1 or publish artifacts.
